description: Decision-grade content ontology for packet-scoped RAWR architecture authority extraction.

entity_types.description: A source document participating in architecture authority.
A document or rule set that defines authority precedence.
A named layer or plane in the RAWR architecture.
A top-level semantic kind such as service, plugin, app, resource, provider, or runtime.
A package, folder, module, generated artifact, or runtime component.
A public package name, import surface, or published SDK surface.
A named authoring, startup, compilation, mounting, or runtime operation.
A runtime plan, graph, assembly, access object, bundle, catalog, diagnostic, or telemetry artifact.
A semantic, runtime, subsystem, native framework, or control-plane boundary.
A runtime realization lifecycle phase.
A runtime role such as server, async, web, cli, agent, or desktop.
A projected surface lane or mountable runtime surface.
A resource capability contract or required runtime capability.
A provider implementation or provider selection concept.
A schema, payload, or typed contract.
A diagnostic, telemetry, catalog, or observation record.
A proof, check, completion gate, or acceptance evidence gate.
A stale target term or pattern that must not survive as target architecture.
A mapping from stale target language to canonical target language.
A topic whose detailed specification is explicitly reserved.
A normative or descriptive architecture claim.
A relevant architecture concept that does not fit a narrower type.

relation_types.description: Subject is authoritative for object within a scope.
Subject replaces object as canonical target language.
Subject forbids object as target architecture.
Subject explicitly does not own object.
Subject is reserved for object or future specification.
Subject is intentionally separate from object.
Subject precedes object in a lifecycle or pipeline.

diagnostic_relation_types.description: Non-decision diagnostic mention; never emitted as a decision graph edge.
